data.author: FlorianReimold
data.message:
eCAL v5.5.0:
* eCAL message recorder released
  * records messages in realtime in HDF5 format
  * synchronized recording over multiple targets
  * pre-buffer feature
  * modern QT based control gui
* eCAL message player
  * eCAL message replay
  * topic filtering
  * adjustable replay speed / looped play
  * modern QT based gui
  * command line version (with interactive mode)
* eCAL HDF5 recording read / write C++ SDK
  * high level API to iterate over recorded data sets
    * auto merging of splitted record files
    * distributed recording on multiple machines, handling as one recording
* Performance improvements
  * reduced memory copying on subscriber side
  * new latency send / receive samples demonstrating new performance
    using callback receive method
    * see Performance section in Readme.md file
    * performance factor 2 for windows factor 4 Linux for large messages
* CPack installer generates start menu entries on windows platform
* Different bug fixes

data.author: patrick-fleischmann-conti
data.message:
FIX: Multiply Defined Symbols (#749)

Fix multiply defined symbols linker error that occurs when
IMeasurement or OMeasurement is used in two different translation
units as the present method definition in the two header files
violates the One Definition Rule.

data.author: johngladp
data.message:
Configuration overwrite keys are now applied to the default configuration when an ini file fails to be loaded. (#61)

Support usage without an ini configuration file on disk.  When an empty string is provided in the "ecal-ini-file" command line parameter default settings are used but the overwrite keys are ignored.  This change moves the processing of the overwrite keys to after the ini file load attempt has occurred; the overwire keys are now applied regardless of the success of loading an ini file.  This permits an application to specify an empty value for the "ecal-ini-file" and thus configure ecal entirely within the application.

Added the "/_vs_out" and ".vs/" folder to the .gitignore file to better support building using visual studio 2019's folder open cmake build flow (which works rather well presently).

Work around for daft Visual Studio 2019 (16.6.2) bug that causes build errors to be reported when there aren't any due to the presence of the word "error" in a cmake message.
